perturbation.original_text: You have requested information regarding lmo Industries Inc. (the "Company", "us" or "we") in connection with your consideration of a possible negotiated transaction with the Company (a "Possible Transaction").
perturbation.changed_text: You have requested information regarding lmo Industries Inc. (the "Corporation", "us" or "we") in connection with your consideration of a possible negotiated transaction with the Company (a "Possible Transaction").
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Company' has been replaced with 'Corporation' in the introduction, but the document consistently refers to 'the Company' throughout. This creates confusion as to whether 'Company' and 'Corporation' are intended to mean the same entity. This contradicts the initial definition and could lead to disputes over which term applies in specific clauses. This impacts the interpretation of the entire agreement, but particularly where obligations are assigned to 'the Company'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of our furnishing you with the Evaluation Materials (as defined below) you agree as follows: CONFIDENTIALITY OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You and your representatives (as defined herein) will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either we or our financial advisor, Credit Suisse First Boston Corporation ("CSFB"), or our other representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials").
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Company' and the changed text defines 'Corporation,' while the contradicted text consistently refers to 'the Company.' This creates a direct contradiction because the document now uses two different terms ('Company' and 'Corporation') seemingly interchangeably to refer to the same entity without clarifying if they are indeed synonymous. This contradicts the initial definition and creates ambiguity, making it unclear which entity is bound by the confidentiality obligations in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: You and your representatives will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential and will not disclose the Evaluation Materials, in any manner, in whole or in part; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you and shall agree to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: You and your representatives will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Potential Deal. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential and will not disclose the Evaluation Materials, in any manner, in whole or in part; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you and shall agree to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Possible Transaction' is changed to 'Potential Deal' in the first sentence of this section. The rest of the section continues to use 'Possible Transaction'. This raises the question of whether the restrictions apply equally to a 'Potential Deal' as to a 'Possible Transaction', creating ambiguity and impacting the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential and will not disclose the Evaluation Materials, in any manner, in whole or in part; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you and shall agree to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Possible Transaction' to 'Potential Deal' in the modified text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text repeatedly refers to 'Possible Transaction' when specifying to whom the Evaluation Materials may be disclosed, and how confidentiality is to be maintained. By changing the initial definition to 'Potential Deal', it creates uncertainty and ambiguity as to whether the same conditions apply to evaluating a 'Potential Deal' as they do a 'Possible Transaction'. Because of this difference, the modified and contradicted sections conflict, because they rely on different terms. As the perturbation definition states, misaligned terminology leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to <PAGE> 2 disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company (at the Company's expense) in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that you or any of your personnel receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to <PAGE> 2 disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company (at the Company's expense) in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'representatives' is replaced by 'personnel' in relation to those receiving a legal request. Later in the same sentence, the obligation to notify falls on 'your representatives'. This creates uncertainty: Does the notification obligation extend to all 'personnel', or only those specifically defined as 'representatives'? The ambiguity weakens the company's ability to ensure notification of potential disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to <PAGE> 2 disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company (at the Company's expense) in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'representatives' with 'personnel' in the context of receiving a legal request. However, the obligation to notify the company remains with 'your representatives'. Since 'personnel' is a broader term than 'representatives', this creates an ambiguity: it is unclear whether all personnel who receive a request are obligated to notify the company, or only those who qualify as 'representatives'. This ambiguity weakens the company's ability to ensure notification of potential disclosures, contradicting the original text which clearly stated the notification obligation applied to 'representatives'. The contradicted text and changed text are in the same location but have different implications now.

perturbation.original_text: NON-DISCLOSURE The disclosure of your possible interest in negotiating a transaction with the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business if for any reason an agreement is not consummated. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that you have entered into this Agreement, that Evaluation Materials have been made available or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: NON-DISCLOSURE The disclosure of your possible interest in negotiating a business deal with the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business if for any reason an agreement is not consummated. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that you have entered into this Agreement, that Evaluation Materials have been made available or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.explanation: In the NON-DISCLOSURE section, 'transaction' is initially described as a 'business deal,' but then reverts to 'Possible Transaction'. If a 'business deal' encompasses activities beyond a 'Possible Transaction' as originally defined, the initial sentence of this section may be interpreted to apply more broadly, potentially prohibiting disclosures related to a wider array of activities than initially intended by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that you have entered into this Agreement, that Evaluation Materials have been made available or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'transaction' with 'business deal' in the Non-Disclosure section's first sentence. The explanation states this potentially broadens the scope of prohibited disclosures beyond 'Possible Transaction' as defined elsewhere. Since the contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Possible Transaction,' using 'business deal' in the changed text creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction, indicating that the non-disclosure agreement may apply to a broader set of activities than intended, leading to confusion about the specific obligations, which meets the definition of misaligned terminology.

perturbation.original_text: RETURN OF DOCUMENTS Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Materials, you and your representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: RETURN OF DOCUMENTS Upon the Corporation's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Materials, you and your representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Here we are using the in-text contradiction mentioned in the first perturbation, where 'Company' and 'Corporation' where being use interchangebly without defining them. This contradiction makes it unclear on what entity can request the return of the documents, the 'Corporation', or the 'Company'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Company' to 'Corporation' in the changed text introduces ambiguity because the document now uses both terms ('Company' and 'Corporation') interchangeably without defining them separately. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as described in the Perturbation Definitions. The contradicted text only uses 'Company', so the use of 'Corporation' in the changed text creates a strong contradiction regarding which entity can request the return of documents. The explanation explicitly highlights this ambiguity as the reason for the contradiction, which aligns with the definitions given. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until one year from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or 2 <PAGE> 3 policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.changed_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until one year from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Business (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or 2 <PAGE> 3 policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Company' has been replaced with 'Business' in the introduction to this section, but then uses 'Company' to refer to who's assets and voting rights the other party can not propose to acquire. This creates a contradiction of who's Board of Directors approval is needed, the Business or the Company. This leads to uncertainity, and contradicts what the initial intention might have been.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree that until one year from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or 2 <PAGE> 3 policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Business' in the introductory clause of the STANDSTILL provision, but the subsequent clauses still refer to the 'Company'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the other party needs approval from the Board of Directors of the 'Business' or the 'Company' for the actions described. This directly contradicts the intention of the contradicted text which specifies needing approval from the Board of Directors of the 'Company'.
